validationCommand: python -c "import torch; assert hasattr(torch,'xpu') and torch.xpu.is_available(); from src.optimization.memory_manager import is_xpu_available, get_gpu_backend; from src.common.distributed.basic import get_device; assert is_xpu_available(); assert get_gpu_backend()=='xpu'; assert get_device().type=='xpu'; print('SeedVR2 XPU OK')"
knownIssues: memory_manager.py get_device_list() only enumerates CUDA and MPS devices — XPU cards are invisible to the SeedVR2 runtime, so all device selection defaults to CPU or fails. (Fixed by patches/seedvr2-xpu-registration.patch, already applied upstream on this fork as commit f183a05.)

video_upscaler.py output conversion checks `sample.is_cuda or sample.is_mps` but not `is_xpu`, so tensors on XPU never get moved to CPU for ComfyUI output — silent data loss or crash. (Fixed, same commit as above.)

get_basic_vram_info() uses torch.cuda.mem_get_info() with no XPU branch, so VRAM checks return 'No GPU backend available' on Intel systems. (Fixed, same commit — this fork queries real torch.xpu VRAM stats, not a system-memory proxy like Intel's own llm-scaler-omni patch for this same package does.)

Workflow widgets hardcode `cuda:0` for SeedVR2LoadDiTModel and SeedVR2LoadVAEModel — must be changed to `xpu:0` or `cpu` via runtime policy. Still requires a per-workflow runtime-policy change; not something a node-package patch can fix.

generation_phases.py's VRAM-offload eligibility checks (encode_all_batches, upscale_all_batches) only tested is_cuda/is_mps, so latents were never offloaded on XPU, risking VRAM accumulation across batches. Fixed in patches/seedvr2-xpu-delta-fixes.patch, cross-referenced from Intel's llm-scaler-omni SeedVR2 patch.
model_loader.py's checkpoint-load allocator-error fallback (CPU-intermediate load) only handled MPS, not XPU. Fixed in the delta patch.

The three resize transforms (area/na/side_resize.py) only guarded MPS for the torchvision antialias-unsupported bug and bilinear-interpolation-forcing; XPU has the same limitation and was unguarded. Fixed in the delta patch.

compatibility.py's NaDiT parameter/buffer dtype unification and RoPE-freqs FP8-to-compute-dtype conversion only ran under the MPS no-autocast-fallback branch; XPU needs the identical treatment and was skipped, risking dtype-promotion errors. Fixed in the delta patch.

distributed/basic.py's get_device() (multi-GPU rank device selection) had no XPU branch, always falling back to a CUDA device string. Fixed in the delta patch — note Intel's own llm-scaler-omni patch for this exact fix has a real bug (inserts a second get_device() definition as dead code, shadowed by the original); do not copy that patch literally, re-derive the single-function fix instead.

compatibility.py imports FlashAttention/SageAttention unconditionally; these are NVIDIA-only. This fork already guards flash_attn import safely (ensure_flash_attn_safe stubs it); the optimization-status print line was updated to show only Triton availability on XPU (cosmetic, not a correctness fix).

workarounds.action: Apply patches/seedvr2-xpu-registration.patch to the SeedVR2 custom node package (base XPU device registration). Then apply patches/seedvr2-xpu-delta-fixes.patch (VRAM-offload checks, resize/antialias, dtype conversion, get_device) on top. Both are already applied and committed directly to this environment's installed copy (tinafengfun/comfyui_seedvr2 fork, commits f183a05 and dacc179).
workarounds.rationale: These are the minimal changes to make SeedVR2 fully device-aware on Intel XPU, closing gaps found by cross-referencing Intel's own llm-scaler-omni SeedVR2 XPU patch against this fork's actual state.
workarounds.tradeoff: Must be re-applied (or re-derived) if the SeedVR2 custom node package is updated past this base version. Patches are against the package's own source (src/), not ComfyUI core.

workarounds.action: Change workflow widget values for SeedVR2LoadDiTModel and SeedVR2LoadVAEModel from `cuda:0` to `xpu:0` (or `cpu` if VRAM is insufficient). Record this as a runtime-policy change.
workarounds.rationale: The workflow JSON hardcodes CUDA device selection. Even with the patch, the device must be explicitly set to XPU.
workarounds.tradeoff: Requires a deliberate workflow modification — do not silently edit. Must be recorded as a migration decision.

workarounds.action: If VRAM headroom is insufficient for full-resolution upscaling (SeedVR2 target can be 3840×7680), run a reduced-scope branch smoke first (e.g. 256×256 input), then scale up.
workarounds.rationale: SeedVR2 DiT intermediate tensors can be 4-8× the input resolution. On XPU with <16GB VRAM, full-resolution will OOM.
workarounds.tradeoff: Slower validation cycle; full-resolution run deferred until memory math justifies it.

validationEvidence: Zimage/04-source-audit.md; direct import test on remote-124-12 confirming is_xpu_available()=True, get_gpu_backend()='xpu', get_device()=xpu:0, and successful import of all patched modules (2026-07-20)
retireCondition: SeedVR2 custom node package (numz/ComfyUI-SeedVR2_VideoUpscaler upstream) adds native XPU device enumeration, VRAM query, resize/dtype handling, and multi-GPU device selection. Track upstream releases and this fork's divergence from them.
